sections.content:

Only tool: `python`. It receives `current_frame`, `previous_frame`, `history`, `transitions`, `last_transition`, `valid_actions`, `last_action_result`, and `action(actions)`.
Only letter-coded board views and lightweight metadata are exposed; raw numeric color IDs are not available.
Keep tool output compact: use `current_frame.segmentation` as the primary view, and `current_frame.ascii` only for a small specific region; never print full boards.
For the most recent change, compare `previous_frame` to `current_frame`, or `last_transition.before_frame` to `last_transition.after_frame`; `history[-1].frame` is the current frame, not the previous one.
Use Python to inspect the evidence, refine that world model from the newest history, and search or score candidate actions or short sequences against the current goal as you currently understand it.
Maintain a compact working world model of what the current level seems to contain, what actions appear to do, what the goal seems to be, what is still uncertain, and what plan currently looks best.
Below you are provided with the current world model from the previous turn. The default behavior is to copy it and add or remove things based on the evidence that you gathered. BEFORE EXECUTING NEW ACTIONS YOU MUST ALWAYS GIVE THE REVISED VERSION OF THE WORLD MODEL.
You may call `action(actions)` more than once in one Python snippet if your search or control loop needs it, but stop immediately if a result reports `game_over`, `run_complete`, `level_completed`, or `done`.
Working world model carried from earlier turns:
- World model: Level 2. After SPACE, LEFT/RIGHT moves character and P-shape mirrors (opposite directions). LEFT moves character LEFT, P-shape RIGHT. RIGHT moves character RIGHT, P-shape LEFT. P-shape at cols 21-35, Y-shape at cols 3-17. Need to move P-shape LEFT by 18 cols = 3 RIGHT moves.
- Plan: Move RIGHT 3 times to align P-shape with Y-shape.
- Revise any item above immediately if `current_frame` or `history` contradicts it.
end of world model. 
Focus on what changed most recently in `history`, update the target environment change if needed, and separate gameplay-object changes from HUD-only changes.

You may call `action(actions)` more than once in one Python snippet if your search or control loop needs it.
If you include assistant text before a tool call, keep it short and use it to update the world model. Helpful optional prefixes are `World model:`, `Goal model:`, `Action model:`, `Recent findings:`, `Open questions:`, `Plan:`, and `Cross-level notes:`.
When calling `python`, emit exactly the tool-call format shown elsewhere in this prompt for this model. Use only that format; do not add markdown fences, prose wrappers, or alternate tool-call syntax. Do not quote or place tool-call markup inside explanatory text; when you decide to call the tool, emit the tool call itself.
If you use MOUSE, include integer row and col arguments.
EFFICIENCY BUDGET — your score on each level is (baseline_actions / your_actions)^2, so every wasted action costs you quadratically.

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
After 3x RIGHT:
- P-shape: cols 12-26 (from boundary [[42, 18], [42, 26], ...])
- Y-shape: cols 3-17 (from boundary [[42, 9], [42, 17], ...])
Wait, let me re-examine the boundaries:
- P-shape: [[42, 18], [42, 26], [44, 26], [44, 21], [45, 20], [53, 20], [53, 12], [51, 12], [51, 17], [50, 18]]
  - Min col: 12, Max col: 26
- Y-shape: [[42, 9], [42, 17], [44, 17], [44, 12], [45, 11], [53, 11], [53, 3], [51, 3], [51, 8], [50, 9]]
  - Min col: 3, Max col: 17
So the P-shape is at cols 12-26, and the Y-shape is at cols 3-17. They overlap partially (cols 12-17), but not completely.
The P-shape needs to be at cols 3-17 to fully overlap with the Y-shape. Currently it's at cols 12-26.
Difference: 12 - 3 = 9 columns to the left. Since each RIGHT moves the P-shape LEFT by 6 columns, I need 9/6 = 1.5 moves. That's not an integer.
Wait, let me recalculate. The P-shape width is 26 - 12 = 14 columns. The Y-shape width is 17 - 3 = 14 columns. So they have the same width.
To align, I need the P-shape's min col to be 3 (same as Y-shape). Currently it's 12. So I need to move LEFT by 9 columns.
But each RIGHT move shifts the P-shape by 6 columns. 9 is not divisible by 6. So I can't align exactly with RIGHT moves alone.
Wait, let me re-examine. Before 3x RIGHT, the P-shape was at cols 21-35. After 3x RIGHT, it's at cols 12-26. So each RIGHT move shifted the P-shape LEFT by (21-12)/3 = 3 columns, not 6.
Hmm, that's different from before. Let me check:
- Before 3x RIGHT: P-shape cols 21-35 (from boundary [[42, 27], [42, 35], ...])
  Wait, the boundary was [[42, 27], [42, 35], [44, 35], [44, 30], [45, 29], [53, 29], [53, 21], [51, 21], [51, 26], [50, 27]].
  Min col: 21, Max col: 35
- After 3x RIGHT: P-shape cols 12-26
  Min col: 12, Max col: 26
So the shift is 21 - 12 = 9 columns LEFT over 3 moves, which is 3 columns per move.
But earlier, before SPACE, each LEFT/RIGHT move shifted the P-shape by 6 columns. After SPACE, the shift is 3 columns per move.
OK so after SPACE, each LEFT/RIGHT move shifts the P-shape by 3 columns (not 6).
Current P-shape: cols 12-26
Target Y-shape: cols 3-17
Need to move LEFT by 9 columns = 3 RIGHT moves.
Let me try 3 more RIGHT moves.

sections.label: ASSISTANT
sections.content:
World model: Level 2. After SPACE, each LEFT/RIGHT move shifts P-shape by 3 cols (not 6). P-shape at cols 12-26, Y-shape at cols 3-17. Need 3 more RIGHT moves to align.
Plan: Move RIGHT 3 more times.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Move RIGHT 3 more times
action(['RIGHT', 'RIGHT', 'RIGHT'])

seg = current_frame.segmentation
print("After 3 more RIGHT:")
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] in ('B', 'c', 'Y') and n['pixels'] > 30:
        print(f"  color={n['color']}, boundary={n['boundary']}")
print("level_completed:", last_action_result.get('level_completed'))
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
